chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Welcome back to the Sigma Web Development course. In this video, we are going to learn about CSS Flexbox.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Flexbox is a one-dimensional layout method for arranging items in rows or columns.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Items flex to fill additional space or shrink to fit into smaller spaces.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: To use flexbox, you need to set display flex on the parent container.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The flex-direction property defines in which direction the container wants to stack the flex items.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The row value stacks the flex items horizontally from left to right.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The column value stacks the flex items vertically from top to bottom.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The row-reverse value stacks the flex items horizontally but from right to left.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Justify-content is used to align the flex items along the main axis.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The center value aligns the flex items at the center of the container.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Space-between distributes items evenly, first item is on the start line, last item on the end line.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Space-around distributes items evenly with equal space around each item.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Align-items is used to align the flex items along the cross axis.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The stretch value stretches the flex items to fill the container.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The flex-wrap property specifies whether the flex items should wrap or not.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The wrap value makes the items wrap onto multiple lines from top to bottom.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The flex property is a shorthand for flex-grow, flex-shrink, and flex-basis.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Flex-grow specifies how much a flex item will grow relative to the rest of the flex items.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Flex-shrink specifies how much a flex item will shrink relative to the rest.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: The gap property is used to set the gap between flex items, it is very useful.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Align-self is used to override the align-items value for specific flex items.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Order property specifies the order of the flex items in the container.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Let me show you a practical example. We will create a navigation bar using flexbox.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: First create a nav element and add some links inside it.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Now set display flex on the nav and use justify-content space-between.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: This is the power of flexbox - with just two properties we created a responsive nav.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Flexbox is very important and is used in almost every modern website.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: In the next video, we will learn about CSS Grid which is for two-dimensional layouts.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Practice flexbox as much as you can, it will make your CSS life very easy.

chunks.title: CSS Flexbox - Ultimate MasterClass
chunks.text: Thank you for watching. Like this video and I will see you in the next one.

text: Welcome back to the Sigma Web Development course. In this video, we are going to learn about CSS Flexbox. Flexbox is a one-dimensional layout method for arranging items in rows or columns. Items flex to fill additional space or shrink to fit into smaller spaces. To use flexbox, you need to set display flex on the parent container. The flex-direction property defines in which direction the container wants to stack the flex items. The row value stacks the flex items horizontally from left to right. The column value stacks the flex items vertically from top to bottom. The row-reverse value stacks the flex items horizontally but from right to left. Justify-content is used to align the flex items along the main axis. The center value aligns the flex items at the center of the container. Space-between distributes items evenly, first item is on the start line, last item on the end line. Space-around distributes items evenly with equal space around each item. Align-items is used to align the flex items along the cross axis. The stretch value stretches the flex items to fill the container. The flex-wrap property specifies whether the flex items should wrap or not. The wrap value makes the items wrap onto multiple lines from top to bottom. The flex property is a shorthand for flex-grow, flex-shrink, and flex-basis. Flex-grow specifies how much a flex item will grow relative to the rest of the flex items. Flex-shrink specifies how much a flex item will shrink relative to the rest. The gap property is used to set the gap between flex items, it is very useful. Align-self is used to override the align-items value for specific flex items. Order property specifies the order of the flex items in the container. Let me show you a practical example. We will create a navigation bar using flexbox. First create a nav element and add some links inside it. Now set display flex on the nav and use justify-content space-between. This is the power of flexbox - with just two properties we created a responsive nav. Flexbox is very important and is used in almost every modern website. In the next video, we will learn about CSS Grid which is for two-dimensional layouts. Practice flexbox as much as you can, it will make your CSS life very easy. Thank you for watching. Like this video and I will see you in the next one.
